item.title: Falling into a black hole
item.description: In this video I show you what it actually looks like to drop different things into a black hole! I talk about gravitational lensing, gravitational time dilation and gravitational red shifting. All of this to show you what it would actually look like to watch something fall into a black hole.

media:content.media:title: Falling into a black hole
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you what it actually looks like to drop different things into a black hole! I talk about gravitational lensing, gravitational time dilation and gravitational red shifting. All of this to show you what it would actually look like to watch something fall into a black hole.

item.title: Powder reaction
item.description: Why a powder behaves this way in a simple science demonstration.

media:content.media:title: Powder reaction
media:content.media:description: Why a powder behaves this way in a simple science demonstration.

item.title: Indoor skydiving explains rocket motion
item.description: In this video I go indoor skydiving in order to answer a very interesting question! If you have a ball (or person) floating above a high strength fan on a scale, does the scale feel the weight of the ball or person?

media:content.media:title: Indoor skydiving explains rocket motion
media:content.media:description: In this video I go indoor skydiving in order to answer a very interesting question! If you have a ball (or person) floating above a high strength fan on a scale, does the scale feel the weight of the ball or person?

item.title: Exposing the fortune teller "miracle" fish!
item.description: The Action Lab explores the fortune teller fish and looks at the science behind the trick.

media:content.media:title: Exposing the fortune teller "miracle" fish!
media:content.media:description: The Action Lab explores the fortune teller fish and looks at the science behind the trick.

item.title: The volcano reaction you didn't do in school
item.description: I do a volcano experiment that produces heat and ash just like a real volcano

media:content.media:title: The volcano reaction you didn't do in school
media:content.media:description: I do a volcano experiment that produces heat and ash just like a real volcano

item.title: The reverse balloon experiment
item.description: In this video I show you how to make a reverse balloon. A balloon that you can inflate by sucking and not blowing! I show you how this works and then show you how it relates to our lungs.

media:content.media:title: The reverse balloon experiment
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you how to make a reverse balloon. A balloon that you can inflate by sucking and not blowing! I show you how this works and then show you how it relates to our lungs.

item.title: Cutting glass with scissors
item.description: In this video I show you how it is possible to cut glass with scissors using water!

media:content.media:title: Cutting glass with scissors
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you how it is possible to cut glass with scissors using water!

item.title: Instant pickles
item.description: How pickles are made in a vacuum chamber using science and pressure.

media:content.media:title: Instant pickles
media:content.media:description: How pickles are made in a vacuum chamber using science and pressure.

item.title: Seeing photosynthesis in real time
item.description: In this video I show you a really neat experiment that lets you actually see photosynthesis happening in front of your eyes. I use spinach leaf disks soaked in baking soda that have had the air sucked out of them, then I shine light on them in order to induce photosynthesis. The leaves then make oxygen which makes them float in water due to the increased buoyancy.

media:content.media:title: Seeing photosynthesis in real time
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you a really neat experiment that lets you actually see photosynthesis happening in front of your eyes. I use spinach leaf disks soaked in baking soda that have had the air sucked out of them, then I shine light on them in order to induce photosynthesis. The leaves then make oxygen which makes them float in water due to the increased buoyancy.

item.title: A real-life infinite staircase
item.description: In this video I show you a 3d printed infinite staircase also called an Escherian staircase after M.C Escher. I talk about how this staircase can actually be possible in Nil space using non-euclidean geometry.

media:content.media:title: A real-life infinite staircase
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you a 3d printed infinite staircase also called an Escherian staircase after M.C Escher. I talk about how this staircase can actually be possible in Nil space using non-euclidean geometry.

item.title: Can two magnets orbit each other?
item.description: The Action Lab explores whether two magnets can orbit each other in this simple physics experiment.

media:content.media:title: Can two magnets orbit each other?
media:content.media:description: The Action Lab explores whether two magnets can orbit each other in this simple physics experiment.
